questions.text: The property of metals by which they can be beaten into thin sheets is called:
questions.explanation: Malleability is the physical property that allows metals to be hammered or rolled into thin sheets without breaking.

questions.text: Which of the following metals are the best conductors of heat?
questions.explanation: Silver is the best conductor of heat, followed closely by copper. Lead and mercury are relatively poor conductors.

questions.text: Which metal exists as a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature. Bromine is a non-metal that is liquid.

questions.text: Metals like Sodium and Potassium are stored in kerosene because:
options: They react vigorously with air and moisture
questions.explanation: Sodium and Potassium are highly reactive and catch fire if kept in the open due to reaction with moisture and oxygen.

questions.text: Which non-metal is lustrous in appearance?
questions.explanation: Although non-metals are generally dull, Iodine is an exception as it has a shiny, lustrous surface.

questions.text: The hardest natural substance known is an allotrope of Carbon called:
questions.explanation: Diamond is an allotrope of carbon and is the hardest known natural substance.

questions.text: Metal oxides that react with both acids and bases to produce salt and water are called:
questions.explanation: Oxides like Al2O3 and ZnO show both acidic and basic behavior and are known as amphoteric oxides.

questions.text: Which of the following pairs will give displacement reactions?
questions.explanation: Copper is more reactive than silver and can displace silver from silver nitrate solution.

questions.text: When Calcium reacts with water, it starts floating because:
options: Bubbles of hydrogen gas stick to its surface
It forms a light oxide layer
questions.explanation: Calcium reacts with water to form hydrogen gas; these bubbles stick to the metal, causing it to float.

questions.text: Aqua regia is a freshly prepared mixture of concentrated HCl and concentrated HNO3 in the ratio:
questions.explanation: Aqua regia (Royal Water) consists of 3 parts concentrated Hydrochloric acid and 1 part concentrated Nitric acid.

questions.text: In the reactivity series, which metal is placed at the top?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) is the most reactive metal in the standard reactivity series taught in NCERT.

questions.text: Which property is NOT generally shown by ionic compounds?
questions.explanation: Ionic compounds do not conduct electricity in solid state because ions are fixed in a rigid structure; they conduct in molten or aqueous state.

questions.text: The bond formed by the transfer of electrons from a metal to a non-metal is known as:
questions.explanation: An ionic (or electrovalent) bond is formed by the complete transfer of one or more electrons from one atom to another.

questions.text: The earth's crust is a major source of metals. Elements or compounds occurring naturally in the crust are called:
questions.explanation: Minerals are naturally occurring inorganic substances. Ores are minerals from which metals can be extracted profitably.

questions.text: Ores are usually contaminated with large amounts of impurities like soil and sand, called:
questions.explanation: Gangue refers to the unwanted rocky or sandy impurities present in an ore.

questions.text: Sulphide ores are converted into oxides by heating strongly in the presence of excess air. This process is:
questions.explanation: Roasting is used for sulphide ores. Calcination is used for carbonate ores (heating in limited air).

questions.text: Which of the following metals is extracted by electrolysis of its molten chloride?
questions.explanation: Highly reactive metals like Sodium, Magnesium, and Calcium are extracted by electrolysis of their molten salts.

questions.text: The reaction of Iron(III) oxide with Aluminum used to join railway tracks is known as:
questions.explanation: The thermite reaction is highly exothermic, producing molten iron used for welding tracks.

questions.text: In electrolytic refining of copper, the impure metal is made the:
questions.explanation: In electrolytic refining, the impure block is the anode (+), and a thin strip of pure metal is the cathode (-).

questions.text: Galvanisation is a method of protecting iron from rusting by coating it with a thin layer of:
questions.explanation: Zinc is used in galvanisation because it acts as a sacrificial layer, corroding before the iron.

questions.text: An alloy is a ______ mixture of two or more metals, or a metal and a non-metal.
questions.explanation: Alloys are homogeneous mixtures that exhibit improved properties over their constituent elements.

questions.text: The alloy 'Brass' consists of:
questions.explanation: Brass is an alloy of Copper (Cu) and Zinc (Zn). Bronze is Copper and Tin.

questions.text: Which alloy is used for welding electrical wires together?
questions.explanation: Solder, an alloy of Lead (Pb) and Tin (Sn), has a low melting point and is ideal for electrical welding.

questions.text: If one of the metals in an alloy is Mercury, then the alloy is called:
questions.explanation: Amalgams are specific alloys formed when mercury is mixed with other metals.

questions.text: Pure gold (24 carat) is very soft. To make it hard for jewelry, it is alloyed with:
questions.explanation: In India, 22 carat gold is commonly used, meaning 22 parts gold and 2 parts copper or silver.

questions.text: Which of the following metals do not react with water at all?
questions.explanation: Metals like Lead, Copper, Silver, and Gold do not react with water, even as steam.

questions.text: Which gas is generally produced when a metal reacts with dilute acid?
questions.explanation: Metals react with acids to produce salt and hydrogen gas (with exceptions like HNO3).

questions.text: Hydrogen gas is not evolved when a metal reacts with Nitric acid (HNO3) because:
options: HNO3 is a strong oxidizing agent
Metals do not react with HNO3
questions.explanation: HNO3 oxidizes the H2 produced to water and itself gets reduced to nitrogen oxides.

questions.text: Non-metals generally form ______ oxides when they react with oxygen.
questions.explanation: Non-metal oxides (like SO2, CO2) dissolve in water to form acidic solutions.

questions.text: Which metal is the most ductile, capable of being drawn into a 2km long wire from 1g of metal?
questions.explanation: Gold is the most ductile metal known.

questions.text: Metals generally occur in the earth's crust in which state?
questions.explanation: Unreactive metals like Gold/Silver occur in free state; reactive metals occur as oxides, sulphides, or carbonates.

questions.text: The process of heating carbonate ores in limited supply of air is called:
questions.explanation: Calcination is specifically used for carbonate ores to remove CO2 and moisture.

questions.text: Rusting of iron can be prevented by:
questions.explanation: All these methods create a barrier between the metal surface and air/moisture.

questions.text: Copper reacts with moist CO2 in air to form a green coating of:
questions.explanation: The green layer formed on copper is a mixture of copper carbonate and copper hydroxide.

questions.text: Which of the following is an ionic compound?
questions.explanation: NaCl is formed by the transfer of an electron from Sodium (metal) to Chlorine (non-metal).

questions.text: Metals above Hydrogen in the reactivity series can:
questions.explanation: Metals more reactive than Hydrogen can reduce H+ ions from acids to form H2 gas.

questions.text: In the extraction of metals of medium reactivity (Zn, Fe, Pb), the reducing agent used is usually:
questions.explanation: Metal oxides are reduced to metals by heating them with carbon.

questions.text: Which metal is used in the 'Anodising' process to increase its corrosion resistance?
questions.explanation: Anodising is a process of forming a thick oxide layer of aluminum to make it resistant to further corrosion.

questions.text: The electronic configuration of Sodium (Na) is:
questions.explanation: Sodium has atomic number 11, with 2 electrons in K shell, 8 in L shell, and 1 in M shell.

questions.text: Which property of metals is utilized for making wires?
questions.explanation: Ductility is the ability of a material to be drawn into thin wires.
